raw_consumer_statement:

- Original timeline was departing Split ( XXXX ) at XXXX XXXX arriving to XXXX ( XXXX ) at XXXX XXXX, and then flying from XXXX at XXXX XXXX XXXXo XXXX ( XXXX ). 
- XXXX changed their flights schedule on XX/XX/XXXX and sent a notification. Now the first flight leaves at XXXX XXXX from XXXX and gets to XXXX to XXXX XXXX Obviously its not physically possible to take the second flight that would have already left at XXXX XXXX from XXXX. 
- While airline has made connection physically impossible ( not a customer choice ), XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX are not responding and NOT helping to get any refund, cancellation or resolution. There is NO solution offered whatsoever. 
- I opened a dispute for these 3 changes totalling {$360.00}. 
- XXXX has responded to the dispute - MISSTATING that the flight is still available attaching the ORIGINAL schedule. They FAILED to attached the changed schedule.

However AMEX on multiple occasions has closed my dispute stating that airline declated the flight is available ( based on old schedule ). AMEX is FAILING to review or respond based on the provided the NEW schedule information - dispite multiple times I uploaded this information.

- AMEX is failing to duly review the latest flight schedule information and simply ignores it - basing dispute decision on OLD schedule provided by XXXX. Requesting the NEW schedule to be reviewed and disputes honored with full refund of {$360.00}.
